summary: Completed frontend-only regression validation on Phantom signing patch scope using public preview URL. App shell, markets, topbar controls, notifications modal, settings modal, and status banner remained stable with no UI regressions observed.
action_items: Run one manual Phantom-extension validation for real authorize signing + fallback error message text because browser automation has no Phantom wallet extension.
Run one manual live order-signing check with connected wallet to confirm runtime path uses signTypedDataWithWallet helper in production wallet context.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.js now uses multi-strategy signer helper (ethers signer._signTypedData, eth_signTypedData_v4, eth_signTypedData, and v4 with EIP712Domain fallback), so authorize flow no longer depends on a single direct RPC path.
orders.js order signing now routes through signTypedDataWithWallet helper, aligning order-sign path with authorization compatibility logic.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated against https://quick-fix-71.preview.emergentagent.com from frontend/.env. Confirmed app loads, markets render (topbar markets=100), status banner visible, topbar controls clickable, notifications/settings modals open-close, and nav tabs (Markets/History/Calculators) remain functional. Wallet extension-dependent signing behavior remains untestable in automation.
rca of the issue: Original failure likely stemmed from Phantom typed-data parameter strictness for direct RPC signing. Current patch mitigates by trying multiple compatible typed-data signing strategies and returning a clearer Phantom-specific fallback message if all methods fail.
mocked_api: NO MOCKED APIs used in this testing run
